Module contents

TASK LOADER – Load predefined tasks from YAML configuration

Purpose:

Provides a central interface to load task definitions from config/tasks.yaml. Used by both experiment runner and GUI to ensure consistency.

Author: Deepak Panigrahy

core.utils.get_task_by_id(task_id: str, tasks: List[Dict] | None = None) → Dict | None[source]

Find a task by its ID.

Parameters:

  • task_id – The task identifier (e.g., ‘simple’, ‘capital’)

  • tasks – Optional list of tasks. If None, loads fresh.

Returns:

Task dictionary or None if not found.

core.utils.get_tasks_by_level(level: int, tasks: List[Dict] | None = None) → List[Dict][source]

Filter tasks by complexity level.

Parameters:

  • level – 1, 2, or 3

  • tasks – Optional list of tasks. If None, loads fresh.

Returns:

List of tasks matching the level.

core.utils.list_task_summary(tasks: List[Dict] | None = None) → None[source]

Print a formatted summary of all tasks.

Parameters:

tasks – Optional list of tasks. If None, loads fresh.

core.utils.load_tasks(config_path: str | None = None) → List[Dict[str, Any]][source]

Load tasks from YAML file.

Parameters:

config_path – Path to tasks.yaml. If None, uses default ‘config/tasks.yaml’.

Returns:

List of task dictionaries, each containing id, name, description, level, tool_calls, prompt, tags.

Raises:

  • FileNotFoundError – If config file does not exist.

  • yaml.YAMLError – If YAML parsing fails.
